skill.name: Azure Container Registry SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure Container Registry SDK for Python. Use for managing container images, artifacts, and repositories in Azure ACR. Provides code examples for authentication, repository operations, tag management, manifest handling, and artifact upload/download.
skill.summary: Manage container images and artifacts in Azure Container Registry using the Python SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing reference material and code examples for the Azure Container Registry Python SDK. No executable code, scripts, or dangerous patterns were detected. The skill provides static reference content for SDK usage and presents no security risks.

content.value_statement: Manage container images and artifacts in Azure Container Registry with the official Python SDK. This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for authentication, repository operations, and artifact management.
actual_capabilities: Authenticate to Azure Container Registry using Entra ID or anonymous access
List and manage repositories in Azure ACR
List, filter, and manage container image tags
Upload and download container artifacts and blobs
Clean up old or untagged container images
limitations: Requires azure-containerregistry package to be installed
Requires valid Azure credentials or registry access
Does not create or provision Azure resources
Async client requires proper async/await handling

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Managing CI/CD Pipelines
use_cases.description: Query and manage container images in ACR as part of deployment workflows

use_cases.title: Developer Working with Container Images
use_cases.description: List repositories, inspect image metadata, and manage tags for development projects

use_cases.title: Platform Engineer Maintaining Registry Hygiene
use_cases.description: Clean up old images and manage retention policies for container registries
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers responsible for registry governance

prompt_templates.title: List repositories in ACR
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I list all repositories in my Azure Container Registry using the Python SDK?
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic repository listing

prompt_templates.title: Authenticate with Entra ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate to Azure Container Registry using DefaultAzureCredential in Python

prompt_templates.title: Delete old images by date
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to delete container images older than 30 days from Azure Container Registry
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up old or unused images

prompt_templates.title: Download manifest and blobs
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I download a container manifest and its blob layers from ACR using the Python SDK?
prompt_templates.scenario: Inspecting or backing up container content

output_examples.output:
```python
from azure.containerregistry import ContainerRegistryClient
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

client = ContainerRegistryClient(
    endpoint="https://myregistry.azurecr.io",
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential()
)

for repository in client.list_repository_names():
    print(repository)
```

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=30)

for manifest in client.list_manifest_properties("my-image"):
    if manifest.last_updated_on < cutoff and not manifest.tags:
        print(f"Deleting {manifest.digest}")
        client.delete_manifest("my-image", manifest.digest)
```

best_practices: Use Entra ID (DefaultAzureCredential) for authentication in production instead of storing credentials
Lock production images with can_delete=False to prevent accidental deletion
Delete by digest rather than by tag to avoid orphaned images
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials in source code instead of using managed identities
Deleting images by tag without checking for other tags pointing to the same digest
Using synchronous client for high-volume operations where async would be more efficient

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Container Registry?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production environments. It automatically handles authentication via managed identity, service principal, or developer credentials.

faq.question: Can I access public registries like MCR?
faq.answer: Yes, use anonymous access by setting credential=None and specifying the audience parameter for public registries like mcr.microsoft.com.

faq.question: How do I delete old images safely?
faq.answer: Check manifest.tags before deleting to ensure you are not removing tagged images. Delete by digest to avoid orphaned images.

faq.question: What is the difference between sync and async clients?
faq.answer: Async clients (aio) are recommended for high-throughput operations. They allow concurrent requests and better resource utilization.

faq.question: How do I lock images to prevent deletion?
faq.answer: Use update_manifest_properties or update_repository_properties with can_delete=False to lock images or entire repositories.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure resources?
faq.answer: No, this skill only provides code examples for managing existing Azure Container Registries. You must create registries separately via Azure Portal or CLI.
